findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:11 says 'Fourteen years of marriage had made them experts at the precise weight of what went unsaid.' snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:65 gives the wedding date as 'June 14, 2017.' snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:85 says 'Twelve years of marriage compressed into twenty minutes.' The case number NYC-FAM-2029-041187 (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:83) places the story in 2029. June 2017 to March 2029 is approximately 11 years and 9 months.
findings.problem: The chapter gives two contradictory marriage durations — 'fourteen years' on line 11 and 'twelve years' on line 85 — and neither matches the arithmetic of the stated wedding date (June 14, 2017) against the story year (2029, per the case number). This is an internal self-contradiction and a timeline violation.
findings.rewrite_direction: Reconcile all three data points at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:11, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:65, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:85. If the wedding date stays June 14, 2017, and the story is set in early March 2029, the marriage is approximately 11 years and 9 months. Change line 11 from 'fourteen years' to 'nearly twelve years' or 'twelve years.' Line 85's 'twelve years' can stand as reasonable rounding. Alternatively, if fourteen years is the intended duration, adjust the wedding date to approximately June 2015.
findings.acceptance_test: A single, consistent marriage duration appears throughout the chapter. The number matches the arithmetic of the stated wedding date and the story year (2029) to within a reasonable rounding margin of no more than 6 months. No two references to the marriage's length differ by more than one year.

findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:3 says Mira calculated her subway mark 'her second week on the commute. Fourteen months ago.' snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:23 says 'Fourteen months of the same coffee.' snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:15 says 'She had looked at this photo every morning for fourteen months.' The continuity sheet states: 'The Kesslers have been in proxy roles for about eighteen months.'
findings.problem: The chapter consistently places Mira's proxy tenure at approximately 14 months. The continuity sheet gives approximately 18 months. This is a 3-4 month discrepancy between the chapter and the canonical continuity record.
findings.rewrite_direction: Either update snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:3, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:15, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:23 — changing 'fourteen months' to 'eighteen months' or 'a year and a half' — to match the continuity sheet, or coordinate a continuity-sheet update if 14 months is the intended canonical duration. All three instances must be updated together.
findings.acceptance_test: Every reference to Mira's proxy tenure in the chapter matches the continuity sheet's canonical figure. The same duration appears in all three locations (lines 3, 15, 23) without internal contradiction.

findings.evidence: Three-item lists appear at least 15 times across 140 lines. Examples: 'excellent chairs, excellent monitors, excellent coffee' (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:23); 'data she hadn't gathered about operations she didn't direct to an audience she couldn't see' (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:55); 'Click: the apartment. Click: the savings. Click: the fund' (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:75); 'The notification's exact language. The protocol reference. The response timeline.' (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:133); 'her notes and her laptop and the low sound' (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:137); 'his face, her voice, her handshake' (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:45); 'negotiate, optimize, and execute' (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:47); 'twenty minutes and a blue button and a case number' (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:85).
findings.problem: The tricolon is the chapter's default rhythmic unit. While individual three-item lists are effective (the 'Click' sequence on line 75 earns its repetition), the cumulative effect of 15+ tricolons in 140 lines creates a metronomic three-beat pattern that flattens rhythmic variety and signals autopilot writing.
findings.rewrite_direction: Retain the 5-6 strongest tricolons — those that earn their three beats through escalation or comic timing (e.g., snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:55 'data/operations/audience,' snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:75 'Click' sequence). Convert at least 6 of the remaining tricolons to pairs, single items, or four-item constructions. Target the lower-impact tricolons specifically: snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:23 ('excellent chairs/monitors/coffee'), snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:45 ('face/voice/handshake'), snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:65 ('drop-down menus/checkboxes/fields'), snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:133 ('language/reference/timeline'), snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:137 ('notes/laptop/sound').
findings.acceptance_test: The revised chapter contains no more than 8 tricolon constructions total. No two tricolons appear within the same paragraph. At least 3 of the chapter's listing constructions use a non-three-item count (pairs, quartets, or single-item emphasis).

findings.evidence: 'the way a child carries a blanket' (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:9); 'the way she used to read competitor filings' (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:109); 'the way he responded to the end of his marriage the same way he responded to a delayed train' (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:117); 'the way she understood that Gemini-state's transit notifications weren't sent for her benefit' (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:127); 'the way children sleep when they still believe the floor beneath them is solid' (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:129).
findings.problem: The construction 'the way [subject] [verb]' appears 5 times in 140 lines as the chapter's default simile connector. Each subsequent use reduces the construction's precision and makes the prose feel formulaic.
findings.rewrite_direction: Retain the 2 strongest instances — recommend snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:9 (blanket simile, imagistically vivid) and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:129 (children sleeping, emotionally resonant). Recast the other 3: snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:109 could become a direct comparison ('She read it a third time, slower, with the scrutiny she'd once given competitor filings'); snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:117 could drop the simile framing and render Dan's response directly; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:127 could restructure into a subordinate clause or appositive.
findings.acceptance_test: 'The way [subject] [verb]' appears no more than twice in the chapter. The two retained instances are at least 30 lines apart.

findings.evidence: Two structurally identical tricolon-identity cascades: 'She wasn't the audience. She wasn't the user. She was, apparently, the asset.' (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:113) and 'She was a flag on a petition. She was a line in a queue. She was something the system would get to.' (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:127). Additional 'She was [identity noun]' instances at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:29 ('She was a face attached to an intelligence'), snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:33 ('She was good at this'), and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:135 ('she was a woman who'd been trained to find the angle').
findings.problem: The 'She was/wasn't [identity]' construction appears 7+ times. The two tricolon-identity sequences at lines 113 and 127 are structurally identical — both use three 'She was/wasn't' sentences escalating toward a diminished identity — and their proximity (14 lines apart) makes the second read as a copy of the first's architecture. The pattern reduces each new identity-redefinition to an anticipated formula.
findings.rewrite_direction: Retain ONE tricolon-identity cascade — recommend snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:113 ('audience/user/asset'), which arrives at the sharper insight. Restructure snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:127 to render Mira's diminution through action, sensation, or a different syntactic shape rather than repeating the She was/She was/She was pattern. Reduce total 'She was [identity noun]' constructions to no more than 4 across the chapter by recasting at least 2 of the remaining instances (lines 29, 33, or 135).
findings.acceptance_test: The chapter contains at most one tricolon-identity cascade. Lines 113 and 127 are structurally distinct from each other — different sentence architecture, different rhetorical shape. 'She was [identity noun]' appears no more than 4 times total in the chapter.

findings.evidence: The stretch from snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:25 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:45 is approximately 20 lines of sustained analytical interiority about Mira's competence loss, including the extended 'foreign language' metaphor (line 43), Dan's remembered comment and Mira's analysis of its imprecision (line 45), and multiple paragraphs of essayistic reflection. No scene-level event, physical action, or sensory interruption occurs in this stretch.
findings.problem: The passage reads as a character-study essay rather than a scene. While individual observations are sharp ('Her strategic mind, her pattern recognition... Gemini-state had no use for any of it'), the density of unbroken analysis without embodied interruption makes the prose static. The style bible warns against 'prestige-literary abstractions that haven't been earned by sensory grounding.' The language metaphor at line 43 is abstract and extended beyond what its insight warrants — 4+ sentences elaborating a single conceit.
findings.rewrite_direction: Break the analytical run between snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:25 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:45 by anchoring at least 2 observations in specific physical moments — a sensory detail from the briefing review (the feel of the document, the screen's brightness, a sound from the office), a body sensation during or after the presentation, or a micro-interaction with another proxy. Compress the language metaphor at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:43 from 4+ sentences to 2, letting the image arrive without exhaustive elaboration.
findings.acceptance_test: The stretch from line 25 to line 45 contains at least 2 scene-level moments — physical actions, sensory details, or interpersonal micro-events — that interrupt the analytical register and anchor the interiority in the body. The language metaphor at line 43 is no longer than 3 sentences.

findings.evidence: From snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:91 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:127, Mira discovers the infrastructure flag on her divorce. Her response is rendered almost entirely through analytical processing: reading the notification three times (line 109), searching the protocol document (line 111), recognizing the information architecture (line 113), filling out the contact form (lines 121-123), noting the recursive timeline (line 127). Physical/visceral alarm is limited to 'her hands gone still on the keyboard' and 'the apartment's ambient noise sharpening around her' (line 119).
findings.problem: The chapter's texture mode is 'controlled then alarming.' The proxy workday is beautifully controlled; the notification discovery should shift into alarm. But Mira's response stays in her strategic-analyst register throughout — she processes the shock as information rather than experiencing it as threat. The style bible specifies Mira's stress tells include violent metaphors and clipped syntax. Neither appears in this stretch. The 'alarming' half of the texture mode reads as intellectual puzzle rather than visceral disturbance. The body is underrepresented at the chapter's emotional climax.
findings.rewrite_direction: In the stretch from snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:109 to snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:127, introduce 2-3 moments where Mira's analytical composure cracks: a physical sensation (stomach dropping, temperature change, tightness in chest or throat), a fragmented thought that doesn't complete, or a metaphor that turns violent or physical per her stress profile. The analytical processing can remain dominant — it IS her character — but the body needs to interrupt the mind at least twice. Target especially snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:109 (the initial re-reading, where comprehension should register physically) and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:119 (the recognition of what she can't position against, where the style bible's 'clipped syntax' stress tell should surface).
findings.acceptance_test: The notification discovery section (lines 91-127) contains at least 2 physical/visceral responses beyond 'hands gone still' — one at the point of first comprehension (around line 109) and one at the recognition of systemic opacity (around line 119). At least one metaphor in this section has a violent or physical edge consistent with Mira's stress profile in the style bible.

findings.evidence: 'She carried it to Gemini-state every day the way a child carries a blanket, and she was aware of the analogy, and she did not care' (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:9). 'The consistency should have been comforting, and it was not comforting, and the distance between *should have been* and *was not* was the exact territory her days occupied' (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:23). 'seeming to mean it, which at this point amounted to the same thing' (snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:47).
findings.problem: The prose repeatedly explains or annotates its own images. Line 9's 'and she was aware of the analogy, and she did not care' tells the reader how to read the blanket simile, inoculating against its effect. Line 23's 'the distance between should have been and was not was the exact territory her days occupied' spends more energy analyzing the gap between expectation and reality than rendering it. While this recursive self-commentary is character-consistent (Mira is an analyst), as a prose technique appearing 3 times in 140 lines it domesticates observations that should stay raw. The images are strong enough to land without the appended metacommentary.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:9, cut 'and she was aware of the analogy, and she did not care' — the blanket simile is strong and specific enough to stand alone; Mira's self-awareness can be shown through her behavior rather than narrated. At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:23, compress the 'should have been comforting / was not comforting' construction — let the gap between expectation and reality register through the accumulated detail of the coffee routine rather than through abstract analysis of the gap itself. The chapter can support at most one instance of explicit self-aware metacommentary on its own images — choose the strongest and cut or recast the others.
findings.acceptance_test: The chapter contains no more than 1 instance where the prose explicitly names or analyzes its own metaphor or analogy. The blanket simile at line 9 ends without self-annotating commentary. The coffee-routine observation at line 23 renders the expectation/reality gap through sensory or behavioral detail rather than through abstract naming of the gap.

summary: Chapter 2 lands its required beats with genuine force: Mira's proxy workday at Gemini-state is rendered with physical specificity (the handshake scene, the wrist-washing escape, the empty conference room), the divorce filing achieves its intended mundane gravity, and the infrastructure flag notification is bureaucratically calibrated to produce exactly the right quality of dread. The chapter's central insight — that competence without consequence is a specific, nameable kind of grief — is original and well-earned. However, two HIGH continuity errors require correction: the marriage duration contradicts itself within the chapter (14 years on line 11 vs. 12 years on line 85) and neither figure matches the date math, and Mira's proxy tenure (14 months) contradicts the continuity sheet's canonical 18 months. The prose settles into recurring formulas — tricolon saturation (15+ three-item lists), the 'the way [subject] [verb]' simile connector (5 instances), and a doubled 'She was/wasn't [identity]' tricolon cascade (lines 113 and 127 are architecturally identical). Extended analytical interiority in the competence-grief passage (lines 25-45) reads as essay rather than scene, and the notification discovery renders alarm intellectually rather than viscerally, underdeploying Mira's stress tells from the style bible. The chapter is close to passing on award — the concept, the detail, and several set pieces are strong — but the rhythmic formulas and the flatness of the emotional climax keep it from shortlist-level impact. Dialogue passes: the chapter is intentionally dialogue-sparse per its texture mode, and the minimal dialogue that exists (Dan's remembered line, his text message) is well-calibrated to voice profiles.
